ScanGuru is built by Data Dance Inc. to put explainable, multilingual imaging AI within reach of clinics, hospitals, and universities everywhere — not only the best-resourced ones.

Most imaging AI is built for a handful of modalities and a handful of languages. ScanGuru takes the opposite approach: broad modality coverage, explainable findings, and dual-language reports — the patient’s language alongside English — so a clinic in any market can put AI to work without changing how it already operates.
M.Sc. Digital Signal Processing · NTU Singapore

Data Dance Inc. operates as registered entities in the United States, Australia, and Germany, with a Singapore entity opening shortly — supporting an India-first commercial launch, research collaborations with universities worldwide, and grant partnerships across the US, EU, and Australia.
Dual-language reports in the patient’s language alongside English — a genuine differentiator, not an afterthought.
Our Heal for All program brings free access to verified low-income-country clinics and charity hospitals.
Built as decision support that fits how clinicians already work, with a human confirming every result.
We lead with what is real — training and validation figures, and what is live today versus on the roadmap.
Multilingual, explainable medical imaging AI — dual-language reports in 83 languages.
Market-specific regulatory registration in progress — currently available for research and evaluation.

How we develop and evaluate ScanGuru’s models — and what still lies ahead. We report this honestly: no clearance we haven’t earned, no accuracy figures we can’t stand behind.

Our models use ensembles of established architectures, evaluated internally on held-out data with clinician review. Internal evaluation guides development — it is not the same as independent clinical validation, and we don’t present it as such.
Multiple models per task, combined to improve robustness over a single model.
Findings are surfaced as decision support for a qualified clinician to confirm — the human stays in the loop.
We don’t invent pathology names, confidence numbers, or study results. When we have independent validation data, we’ll publish it here.

All modalities are offered for research use while clinical validation and regulatory work proceed. We are not publishing per-modality sensitivity, specificity, or AUC figures, because we don’t yet have independently validated numbers we’re willing to stand behind.
Building toward the quality management practices expected of medical-device software. Not yet certified.
Pursuing the appropriate route per market (e.g. clinical-decision-support framing where applicable; research/evaluation pilots elsewhere). Nothing filed or cleared yet.
A public performance or clearance claim appears here only once it is genuinely true and evidenced — never before.
We’re looking for research and pilot partners to help generate independent evidence. Join us and help build the data — honestly.
Find answers to common questions about ScanGuru's comprehensive medical imaging AI platform and free plan.
What is the ScanGuru free plan?
The ScanGuru free plan is an early partnership opportunity for medical institutions to deploy our comprehensive medical imaging AI platform ahead of full commercial launch. Pilot partners gain:
Deploy our multilingual imaging AI (50+ pathologies live across 5 modalities, 400+ on the roadmap) during the pilot program
Significant discounts on pilot participation and future licensing
Direct input into feature development and workflow optimization
White-glove implementation and ongoing technical assistance
The pilot phase runs from early 2026 through Q2 2026, with seamless transition to full commercial deployment.
Who can join the free plan?
Monthly imaging volume of 1,000+ scans across multiple modalities
Radiologist champions interested in AI adoption
Willingness to provide feedback and participate in validation
What are the pilot participation requirements?
Monthly check-ins and quarterly detailed feedback sessions
Complete onboarding training for radiologists and technologists
Share de-identified performance metrics and usage data
Participate in independent validation studies (optional but encouraged)
In return, you receive dedicated support, significant pricing advantages, and priority access to new features.
Most institutions are fully operational within 6-8 weeks.
How does ScanGuru integrate with our PACS?
ScanGuru integrates via standard DICOM protocols:
Automatic routing of studies from PACS to ScanGuru
Structured reporting with results sent back to PACS
Major vendors: GE, Philips, Siemens, Canon
Leading PACS: Sectra, Agfa, Fujifilm, McKesson
Cloud PACS: Ambra Health, Life Image, Intelerad
No changes to radiologist workflow - AI results appear directly in your existing viewing environment.
DICOM router or gateway appliance (provided if needed)
Modern web browser for dashboard access
VPN or direct connect options available
Axial, coronal, sagittal reconstructions; thin and thick slice
T1, T2, FLAIR, STIR, DWI sequences across all anatomical regions
All standard DICOM transfer syntaxes supported including compressed formats.
Flagged within 2 minutes of study completion
Processing runs in background - no delay to radiologist workflow.

Help bring AI medical imaging to the clinics that need it most. ScanGuru Ambassadors are volunteers who support our Heal for All mission through outreach, education, and community — in their own regions and languages.

Through our Heal for All program, ScanGuru provides free access to verified low-income-country clinics, children's cancer hospitals, and charity hospitals. Reaching those communities — in the right places, and in the right languages — is something we can't do alone.
Pick the way that fits you. Every role is non-clinical and community-focused.
Connect us with clinics, NGOs, and hospitals in underserved regions that could qualify for free Heal for All access.
Support awareness sessions and share how responsible AI imaging works — with students, community groups, and local healthcare networks.
Help us reach communities in their own language by reviewing our public and educational materials — marketing and community content, never clinical reports.
Introduce us to universities and researchers interested in our research-use pilots.
To keep patients safe and our claims honest, ambassadors never:
Reading images and confirming findings is the work of qualified clinicians — not volunteers.
Ambassadors don't speak to accuracy, diagnosis, or regulatory status on ScanGuru's behalf.
No access to patient images, reports, or any protected health information.
Students, healthcare advocates, community organizers, multilingual volunteers — anyone who wants to help widen access to medical imaging. No technical background required.
Tell us a bit about you and how you'd like to help, and we'll be in touch.

A short form: name, country, type, and what you'd use ScanGuru for.
A quick human review, usually within a few days. We may ask for a registration number or a link.

Ready to bring ScanGuru to your clinic?

Sponsor a clinic — fund a specific, reportable outcome
Most digital-health tools ask funders to underwrite a platform. We ask you to underwrite a clinic — a specific, named facility — and we report back exactly how many scans it ran.
one sponsored clinic = one year of free ScanGuru access for a verified underserved facility = a defined monthly scan allocation, reported back with real usage data drawn straight from our counter.

From academic hospitals to regional clinics

ScanGuru is designed for healthcare systems across the spectrum — major academic centers and regional clinics in emerging markets alike. It is where our multilingual, accessible approach matters most.
Dual-language PDF reports in 83 languages — the patient’s language alongside English — so findings travel across borders and care teams.
Cloud-based and integrated over standard DICOM/PACS and a REST API — no heavy on-site build required to start a pilot.
Free research access and publication collaboration for academic and clinic partners, aligned to our India, Southeast Asia, and Middle East & Africa focus.
From chest X-rays to advanced CT imaging, our AI covers the full spectrum of diagnostic imaging.
Comprehensive CXR analysis detecting 50 pathologies with our 5-model hybrid ensemble. Automated triage, clinical scoring systems, and instant PDF reports.
Rapid detection of intracranial hemorrhage, stroke, and traumatic brain injury with automated measurements and critical alerting.
AI-powered detection of 30+ thoracic pathologies with integrated Lung-RADS and CO-RADS scoring. Lung nodule characterization, COVID-19 patterns, and incidental findings.
AI analysis of panoramic, periapical, and CBCT dental imaging for caries, periodontal disease, and pathology detection.
AI-powered fracture detection and bone abnormality analysis across all skeletal regions with measurement tools.
AI-assisted breast cancer screening with BI-RADS classification, density assessment, and lesion characterization.
Multi-sequence AI analysis for brain, spine, and musculoskeletal MRI with automated measurements and structured reporting.
AI-powered analysis for abdominal, obstetric, and vascular ultrasound with automated measurements.
AI-powered oncology and metabolic imaging analysis with SUV quantification and lesion tracking.
Real reports, built for three audiences

Every analysis produces a structured, explainable PDF — a crisp clinical read for radiologists, a detailed comprehensive report for research and triage, and a plain-language summary for patients. These are real sample reports.
The same report, in any language.

ScanGuru works with clinics, hospitals, and universities through pilots and research collaborations while we complete registration market by market. Here is where we are today — and where we are headed.
We match how ScanGuru is offered to each market’s regulatory pathway — clinical decision support where the framework allows it, and research evaluation where registration is still underway.
In markets where the framework supports it, ScanGuru is available to clinics and hospitals as clinician-reviewed decision support. First markets: India, broader Southeast Asia, and the UAE.
In markets with stricter pathways, we partner on research and evaluation pilots while registration completes. First markets: Singapore, Australia, and Saudi Arabia.
Current as of June 2026 and updated as registrations progress.
Our regulatory roadmap runs alongside our pilots — building the clinical evidence and quality processes each pathway calls for, market by market.
Working toward FDA clearance. Available today through research collaborations.
Working toward CE marking. Available today through research collaborations.
Market registrations progressing across our India, Southeast Asia, and Gulf focus regions.
Every result supports a clinician’s read and is confirmed by a qualified clinician.
We work within the modalities and findings we have trained and validated.
ScanGuru augments the clinical workflow rather than acting as a standalone diagnosis.

ScanGuru is operated by Data Dance Inc., with registered entities in the United States, Australia, and Germany, and a Singapore entity opening shortly. Regulatory engagement is handled per market through the relevant entity.
